title: Cap and terminate the agent loop
description: Every agent loop has an explicit step cap, a terminal-state check, and a defined behavior when the cap is hit.
body: Use when implementing or changing an agent's control loop. Do not run a while-true that exits only when the model says it is done, and avoid relying on the model to choose to stop. Actions: set a maximum step count, check an explicit terminal condition each iteration, and define what happens at the cap (return partial, escalate, fail). Validation signals include a test that a non-converging task halts at the cap rather than looping forever and a trace showing the terminal check each step. Risk boundary: an uncapped loop that can call side-effecting tools is a blocking finding; cap it and gate the side effects.
